title: poa pratensis subsp. alpestris
intro: Poa pratensis subsp. alpestris, also known as alpine meadow grass, is a subspecies of the Kentucky bluegrass plant that is native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Alpine meadow grass can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use.
